role_adherence_notes.linguistic: Correctly identified Spanish text and limited candidates to Spanish-speaking regions.
role_adherence_notes.landscape: Analyzed terrain and vegetation appropriate to the role.
role_adherence_notes.botanics: Identified plant types (Cupressus) and biome characteristics.
role_adherence_notes.regulatory: Analyzed road markings and traffic rules.
role_adherence_notes.meta: Synthesized infrastructure details (poles, trucks) to narrow candidates.

visual_consistency_notes.landscape: Flat terrain and vegetation match the description.
visual_consistency_notes.botanics: Trees visible on the right match the description.
visual_consistency_notes.regulatory: Road markings (double yellow, white edge) are clearly visible.
visual_consistency_notes.meta: Truck and poles are visible and match the description.

verdict.overall_verdict_notes: The agents correctly identified the region (South America) and narrowed down to Argentina/Uruguay. The linguistic agent was weak due to the generic nature of the Spanish text, but the other agents provided strong corroborating evidence for the Southern Cone. The final decision to select Argentina is well-supported by the regulatory and meta evidence.
verdict.region_narrowing_notes: The agents correctly identified the Southern Cone (Argentina/Uruguay) based on landscape, regulatory, and meta evidence. The linguistic agent's broad list was correctly overridden by the more specific visual evidence.
verdict.hypothesis_pool_notes: The pool included Argentina and Uruguay, which are the most plausible candidates. Brazil was a reasonable inclusion given the landscape similarity. The exclusion of other Spanish-speaking countries was justified by the specific visual cues.
